quranicRelevance: Forgetfulness, deliberate abandonment/neglect ('nasoo Allaha fanaseyahum'), and women (Nisa').

keyDerivations.meaning: To forget, neglect, or leave behind

figurativeOrRareUsages: نَسُوا اللَّهَ فَنَسِيَهُمْ (9:67): They neglected/abandoned God, so He abandoned/neglected them (reciprocal abandonment).
discardedNoise: Grammatical classification of Nisa' as plural of Imra'ah without singular root.
Obscure details on forgetting camel tracks.
summaryEn: The root refers to forgetfulness and neglect, denoting both accidental forgetting and the deliberate neglect of divine covenants.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى النسيان والإهمال، دالاً على السهو الطبيعي والإهمال المتعمد للعهود الإلهية.
rawText: النَّسَا [vulg. عِرْقُ النَّسَا, app. The sciatic vein;] the portion, in the thigh, of the vein (عِرْق) which, in the back, is called the وَتِين, and which extends to the shank, where it is called the صَافِن: (IAth, TA, voce أَبْهَرُ:) or the صاَفِن and عرق النسا are two branches of one عِرْق [or vein]: (Ibn-Seenà, vol. i. book iii. p. 608: [where the opening of each of these to let blood is mentioned:]) [in a solid-hoofed animal,] النسا is a vein (عِرْق) proceeding from the hip, or haunch, lying within each thigh, then passing by the hock, so as to reach the hoof: when the breast is fat, each of its thighs becomes cleft by two large portions of flesh, and the نسا runs between them, and is apparent. (S.) [In the present day it seems to be applied by some to the sciatic nerve: and عِرْقُ النَّسَا, as also النَسَا alone, often signifies sciatica, or hip-gout: see نِقْرِسٌ and also شَنِجٌ. تَنَاسَاهُ 6 He pretended that he had forgotten it: (S, KL, * TA:) and (TA) he forgot it; (MA, KL, * TA;) like 🡻نَسِيَهُ: (TA:) [or] he constrained himself to dismiss it from his mind. (MA.) === تُنُوسِىَ It (a word or the like) was forgotten by degrees. (Occurring often in the larger Lexicons.) مُنْسِيهَا for مَنْسِئِهَا: see a verse cited voce عُقْبَةٌ. نَسڤوَ 1 see 6.
